texto: There is a predictable interlude when the rivals suddenly come together and speak for a second of their common loneliness, thus tritely demonstrating that we really are all the same, though I can't think of any really first-rate film, play, or book that isn't unconsciously dedicated to the fact that we are all inconsolably different.
explicacao: Citação de Penelope Gilliat sobre temas ligados a reflexao. “There is a predictable interlude when the rivals suddenly come together and speak for a second of…” articula reflexao, vida, loneliness em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como loneliness e people.

frase_original: There is a predictable interlude when the rivals suddenly come together and speak for a second of their common loneliness, thus tritely demonstrating that we really are all the same, though I can't think of any really first-rate film, play, or book that isn't unconsciously dedicated to the fact that we are all inconsolably different.
